name: My Pad Thai
recipeIngredient: 9 oz Flat rice sticks, 1/8 inch wide
1/3 cup Fresh sweet basil leaves (Thai or purple basil)
2 Red Serrano chili peppers - seeded & very finely minced
1 1/2 large Boned chicken breast halves - cut crosswise into 3/8 inch thick strips OR
1 lb Lean pork - cut into thin slices 3/8 inch by 2 inches
1/4 cup Roasted, unsalted peanuts - coarsely ground
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the vinegar, water, fish sauce, tomato paste, sugar, and dried shrimp in a small bowl; mix until well blended and reserve.
2. Soak the noodles in enough water to cover them for about 30 minutes.
3. In a small skillet or pot, heat vegetable oil to 350 degrees Fahrenheit (or until a dried rice noodle puffs instantly when dropped into the oil).
4. Deep-fry the basil leaves a few at a time, turning them once or twice until crisp and drained on paper towels.
5. Bring the noodles to a boil and cook for 2 minutes, or until almost tender. Drain well and spread on paper towels to dry slightly.
6. Heat vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet. Fry Serrano peppers about 30 seconds, then add garlic and stir-fry until soft.
7. Add chicken or pork and stir-fry until opaque throughout or browned. Stir in shrimp and the sauce; mix completely.
8. Make a well in the center of the mixture and pour in eggs. Scramble evenly when almost set.
9. Add half the noodles, incorporating them thoroughly into the mixture. Stir in remaining noodles and half the bean sprouts.
10. Cook just until bean sprouts are nearly wilted.
11. Divide meat and noodles onto a platter. Top one half with ground peanuts and the other half with uncooked bean sprouts.
12. Arrange lime wedges, cherry tomatoes, and mint sprigs around the noodles and garnish with fried basil.
